---
title: "between_condition_wilcoxon_codon"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-12
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [22, 8, 18, 17, 17, 26]  # aa-col-added
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot (A-site, P-site, E-site, left-to-right) showing per-codon log2 fold-change of BWM-vs-control median per-replicate frequency across the 61 sense codons. Bars sorted by log2_FC high-to-low within each panel; blue = enriched (log2FC >= 0), red = depleted (log2FC < 0). Y-axis range ~-1.2 to +0.7.](../../plots/between_condition/codon/composite/APE_codon_BWM_vs_Control_barplot_composite.png)

Each panel ranks the 61 sense codons by signed log2_FC (BWM median / control median) at one ribosome site. The signed-rank tails are heavier than at AA resolution because many rare codons have median frequencies < 0.005, producing large but unstable |log2_FC|; treat the rightmost few red bars in each panel as `low-count` candidates rather than headline depletions.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `between_condition_wilcoxon_codon.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_condition_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):** `between_condition_wilcoxon_aa.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum (two-sided)
- **Design:** n=6 BWM vs n=6 control replicates (control_d0/d5/d10 reps pooled as 6 control replicates; same pooling for BWM); two-sided MW on per-replicate codon frequencies; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site = own family of 61 sense codons); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_BWM / median_control).

### Family-level caveats

- **timepoint-pooled-confound** — n=6 per condition is built by treating control_d0/d5/d10 reps as 6 control replicates (and similarly for BWM); if the perturbation's effect varies across day_0/5/10, pooling timepoints as noise within each condition masks signal. Both members share this design.
- **mw-floor-tight** — MW two-sided p-floor for n=6 vs n=6 is ~0.00216; per-site BH-FDR families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) make FDR<0.05 feasible but tight.
- **n=6-modest-power** — Mann-Whitney with n=6 vs n=6 has modest power; null result is weakly informative.

---

## Headline

No statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 across 183 codon-level tests (3 sites x 61 sense codons) under Mann-Whitney with BWM (n=6) vs control (n=6) replicates pooled across day_0/5/10. Min `p_adj` = 0.264 — the discrete BH wall (0.00433 x 61 / 1) hit by four cells: **CGG@A** (BWM-depleted, log2_FC = -1.03), **TCA@P** (BWM-depleted, -0.32), **GTA@P** (BWM-depleted, -0.69), and **AGT@E** (BWM-depleted, -0.68). 12 codons clear raw p<0.05 across the 3 sites; all carry `nominal-only`. The codon resolution adds raw-p detail vs the aa file (smallest raw p drops from 0.0260 to 0.00433) but the larger BH family of 61 cancels the gain — FDR<0.05 remains out of reach. Treat as no signal conditional on `mw-floor-tight` + `larger-bh-family` + `timepoint-pooled-confound`; the named near-threshold codons are exploratory leads worth reconciling against per-timepoint Fisher contrasts.

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` (BWM/control median ratio). `p_value` is the raw Mann-Whitney p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected per A/P/E site (family of 61 codon tests). Each site group is split into one sub-table per sign of effect: positive `log2_FC` means BWM-enriched; negative means BWM-depleted. Within each sub-table, rows are up to 5 per direction ranked by raw `p_value` ascending, with `|log2_FC|` descending as the tiebreaker. The `low-count` flag is applied when `min(median_BWM, median_control) < 0.005` (verifiable via `python scripts/dylan_table_checker.py <CSV> --family wilcoxon`). The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ------------ |
| AAG   | K   | +0.206             | 0.0260            | 0.528                | nominal-only |
| AGA   | R   | +0.192             | 0.0649            | 0.710                |              |
| CGT   | R   | +0.205             | 0.0931            | 0.710                |              |
| CTC   | L   | +0.265             | 0.2403            | 0.836                |              |
| TCC   | S   | +0.135             | 0.2403            | 0.836                |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag                    |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------- |
| CGG   | R   | -1.028             | 0.00433           | 0.264                | nominal-only, low-count |
| GTA   | V   | -0.632             | 0.0260            | 0.528                | nominal-only, low-count |
| GAA   | E   | -0.220             | 0.0411            | 0.627                | nominal-only            |
| GCG   | A   | -0.383             | 0.0931            | 0.710                | low-count               |
| GTG   | V   | -0.318             | 0.0931            | 0.710                |                         |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag                    |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------- |
| AAG   | K   | +0.331             | 0.0260            | 0.360                | nominal-only            |
| ATA   | I   | +0.513             | 0.0411            | 0.360                | nominal-only, low-count |
| CGT   | R   | +0.235             | 0.0931            | 0.406                |                         |
| AGA   | R   | +0.274             | 0.1320            | 0.424                |                         |
| AGC   | S   | +0.131             | 0.1320            | 0.424                |                         |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag                    |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------- |
| TCA   | S   | -0.320             | 0.00433           | 0.264                | nominal-only            |
| GTA   | V   | -0.690             | 0.00866           | 0.264                | nominal-only, low-count |
| AGT   | S   | -0.880             | 0.0152            | 0.308                | nominal-only, low-count |
| CCA   | P   | -0.190             | 0.0411            | 0.360                | nominal-only            |
| ACG   | T   | -0.715             | 0.0649            | 0.360                | low-count               |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ------------ |
| CGC   | R   | +0.619             | 0.0411            | 0.728                | nominal-only |
| CGT   | R   | +0.197             | 0.0931            | 0.728                |              |
| CTA   | L   | +0.401             | 0.2403            | 0.728                | low-count    |
| TAC   | Y   | +0.128             | 0.2403            | 0.728                |              |
| TTC   | F   | +0.062             | 0.3095            | 0.728                |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag                    |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------- |
| AGT   | S   | -0.676             | 0.00433           | 0.264                | nominal-only, low-count |
| TCG   | S   | -0.691             | 0.0649            | 0.728                | low-count               |
| GAT   | D   | -0.183             | 0.0931            | 0.728                |                         |
| CGA   | R   | -0.779             | 0.1320            | 0.728                | low-count               |
| CTG   | L   | -0.596             | 0.1320            | 0.728                | low-count               |

### Flag glossary

- `nominal-only` — raw p < 0.05 but `p_adj` >= 0.05. The row would have been flagged significant if tested in isolation, but did not survive BH-FDR correction across its grouping family.
- `low-count` — the codon's count or median frequency is below the test's stability threshold; the effect column for this row is unstable and should not be over-read. At codon level the rare-codon median frequencies in this file span ~0.0002 to ~0.005 — three orders of magnitude below common codons — and large |log2_FC| at these cells is dominated by single-rep fluctuations.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 183
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.2641 (the discrete BH wall: 0.004329 x 61 / 1, hit by CGG@A, TCA@P, GTA@P, AGT@E)
- `min raw-p`: 0.00433 (CGG@A, TCA@P, AGT@E) — three orders of magnitude tighter than the aa file's 0.0260 minimum
- `nominal-only count`: 12 codons across 3 sites with raw p<0.05; none survive BH per-site
- `p_floor`: n/a — user did not flag a floor effect at the CSV level (family-wide `mw-floor-tight` instead)
- Per-site BH families:
  - A site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.264 (CGG, BWM-depleted)
  - P site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.264 (TCA / GTA, BWM-depleted)
  - E site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.264 (AGT, BWM-depleted)

## Biological interpretation

**The file shows no significant differences at FDR<0.05 across 183 codon-level BWM-vs-control comparisons; the codon resolution does not rescue what the AA sister could not see (concrete).** Min `p_adj` is 0.264 — the discrete BH wall at this family size — hit by exactly four cells, all BWM-depleted at or near the smallest raw p at their site (CGG@A, TCA@P, GTA@P, AGT@E). 12 of 183 cells clear raw p<0.05 and all carry `nominal-only`. The AA sister was also a no-signal file; the codon expansion adds three orders of magnitude in raw-p dynamic range (0.026 to 0.00433) but the corresponding ~3x larger BH family cancels the gain on the FDR axis.

**Three of the four BH-wall codons are `low-count` (CGG, GTA, AGT), which is exactly where the codon-level extreme |log2_FC| values cluster (exploratory).** Rare codons sitting below the `< 0.005` median-frequency threshold flagged in the caveats produce volatile log2_FC values dominated by single-rep fluctuations; the right-tail bars in each composite panel (about -1.0 log2) are concentrated on these rows. Mechanistically, an A-site arginine codon depletion (CGG is one of six Arg synonyms) would be consistent with codon-decoding modulation in BWM, but at this count level the cell is more naturally read as sampling noise. The single non-`low-count` BH-wall cell, TCA@P (Ser; `log2_FC` = -0.32), is the most stable of the four near-misses and is the cell to carry forward most carefully.

**Sub-FDR coherence at the A-site points at a low-magnitude lysine-AAG enrichment in BWM (exploratory).** At codon resolution AAG@A is the single A-site enriched hit carrying `nominal-only` (log2_FC = +0.21, raw p = 0.026, `p_adj` = 0.53); the other A-site enriched near-misses are AGA (Arg) and CGT (Arg). The AA sister `between_condition_wilcoxon_aa.csv` is the natural cross-check for whether the same lysine-A-site lead reproduces at AA resolution. This is not a finding — the cell does not clear FDR — but it is the clearest exploratory pattern in the file.

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claims to quote: this file is no-signal at FDR for BWM-vs-control at codon resolution with timepoints pooled; the minimum achievable `p_adj` at this BH family size when one codon hits the empirical p-floor is structurally 0.264. Exploratory patterns worth checking in the per-timepoint Fisher sister test: (1) does TCA@P (the non-`low-count` BH-wall cell, log2_FC = -0.32) reappear with consistent direction at any single timepoint in `per_timepoint_fisher_codon.csv`? (2) does the AAG@A nominal-only A-site enrichment (BWM-direction, log2_FC = +0.21, `p_adj` = 0.53) reappear with consistent direction in `per_timepoint_fisher_codon.csv`, given that the same lysine-A-site lead also shows as the closest-to-significant enriched residue in the AA sister `between_condition_wilcoxon_aa.csv`? Noise to ignore: the three `low-count` BH-wall codons (CGG, GTA, AGT) — their |log2_FC| should not be quoted at the magnitudes printed in the tables.

## Methods

Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum two-sided on per-replicate codon frequencies, n=6 BWM vs n=6 control with reps pooled across day_0/5/10, BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site = own family of 61 codon hypotheses). Effect column is `log2_FC` of medians (`median_BWM/median_control`); test statistic is `U_stat`. The codon resolution is the natural follow-up to `between_condition_wilcoxon_aa.csv` and shares its design. The test answers "is codon X distributed at different per-rep frequencies between BWM and control replicates?", *not* "is codon X enriched at stall sites vs the transcriptome's codon usage" (within-condition binomial) and *not* a comparison vs an external null.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **timepoint-pooled-confound.** n=6 per condition is built by pooling control_d0/d5/d10 (and the matching BWM) reps; if BWM's effect varies across timepoints, that variation is treated as noise — no-signal at this resolution does not rule out a day-localized BWM-vs-control signal, so cross-check `per_timepoint_fisher_codon` before concluding biological absence at any specific codon.
- **mw-floor-tight.** MW exact-test p-floor for n=6 vs n=6 is ~0.00216 and the file's smallest observed raw p is 0.00433 (~2x the floor). The absolute minimum achievable `p_adj` at this BH family size is 0.132 even under the best possible single-codon-on-floor scenario; the observed 0.264 minimum is the practical floor when one codon sits at 0.00433 and the rest are uncorrelated.
- **n=6-modest-power.** Modest power at this n means an effect of biologically meaningful size can be missed; a no-signal result is weakly informative, not strong evidence of equivalence.
- **larger-bh-family.** Per-site BH family expanded from 20 (aa) to 61 (codon), tightening FDR clearance roughly 3x relative to the aa variant; the file's min `p_adj` = 0.00433 x 61 = 0.264 confirms the structural ceiling. The codon resolution's gain in raw-p dynamic range is cancelled by the BH-family-size cost on the FDR axis.
- **low-count-rare-codon-instability.** Rare codons (CGG, GTA, ATA, AGT, GGG, TTA — flagged `low-count` in the tables) have median frequencies < 0.005; the rank-sum is on per-rep frequencies so this does not invalidate the test, but the `log2_FC` column at these rows is unstable and should not be read at the magnitudes printed. Three of the four BH-wall codons sit in this class.
- **weighted_log2_enrichment-absent.** `log2_FC` is the median ratio and does not weight by absolute count; CGG@A (median ~0.0009) and AAG@A (median ~0.09) get the same column real estate despite a 100x abundance difference — when comparing cells in the sub-tables above, weight common-codon rows much more heavily than rare-codon rows.

### Considered and ruled out

- **synonymous-codon-redundancy.** Concern that AA-driven signals would dilute across synonymous codons and look weaker at codon level than at AA, which could mislead if the AA file had shown a clean signal that codon resolution failed to resolve. Ruled out: the AA sister was also no-signal at FDR, so "codon dilution weakening a clean AA signal" is not the operative concern — both resolutions show no signal for the same structural reasons.
- **asymptotic-with-ties.** Concern that scipy may default to the asymptotic Z approximation under tied zero-frequency reps for rare codons, which would shift p-values away from the exact-floor bound. **Ruled out, empirically verified** (see audit in `scripts/_for_claude_mw_branch_audit.py`, invoked as `python scripts/_for_claude_mw_branch_audit.py --level codon`): only 4 of 183 (site, codon) tests have any rank ties in the pooled 12-element sample — all rare codons (ATA@E, CGG@E, TTA@E, TTA@P), all far from raw p<0.05 (smallest p_asym = 0.297). For those 4, `scipy.stats.mannwhitneyu(method='auto')` chose the **asymptotic** branch; for the remaining 179 it chose **exact**. Recomputed p-values match the pipeline CSV to ~1e-16, so the pipeline's reported p-values are already the right scipy auto-branch choice (179 exact + 4 asymptotic). As a counterfactual, forcing `method='asymptotic'` for all 183 tests shifts raw p-values by at most 0.033 (median 0.008), flips **zero** raw-p<0.05 decisions, and leaves the per-site BH conclusion unchanged: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 either way; the asymptotic counterfactual is in fact *more* conservative (min p_adj = 0.50 / 0.36 / 0.50 at A/P/E vs the as-shipped 0.26 / 0.26 / 0.26). The branch choice does not affect any FDR-level conclusion in this file.

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site BWM-vs-control codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: GGT, CTC, CCC, AAG, CGT, AGA, GGA, CGC, ...; right: GGG, TTA, AAA, ACA, TAT, GTA, CGG). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-1.1 to +0.3. Largest depletion at CGG (~-1.03); largest enrichment at GGT (~+0.27). Magnitude spread is roughly 3x the AA-level companion.](../../plots/between_condition/codon/individual/site_A_codon_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)

![P-site BWM-vs-control codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: CGC, ATA, GCG, AAG, AGA, ...; right: GTA, CGG, CGA, ACG, AGT, TTA, GGG). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-1.3 to +0.7. Largest enrichment at CGC (~+0.62); largest depletion at GGG (~-1.27).](../../plots/between_condition/codon/individual/site_P_codon_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)

![E-site BWM-vs-control codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: CGC, CTA, AGG, CTC, CCT, AGA, AAG, ...; right: CTG, GTA, TTT, AGT, ATA, TCG, CGA, CGG). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-1.1 to +0.6. Largest enrichment at CGC (~+0.62); largest depletion at CGG (~-1.13).](../../plots/between_condition/codon/individual/site_E_codon_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)
